ideas.title: Claude is becoming the creative department
ideas.about: Turn yesterday's Claude plus Higgsfield/image momentum into a simple business demo: Claude creates the campaign brief, image asset, and cartoon/video variation for a real small business.
ideas.evidence: Braydon's May 15 Claude plus Higgsfield post is now at 95.8K views, up from 26.5K in the prior brief, with the newer Claude cartoon video already at 27.9K views, 1,098 likes, and 309 shares from May 16 night MT. Justyn's Higgsfield MCP plus Claude post is still a strong wave signal at 57.3K views.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Claude is your creative department now
ideas.spokenHook: Claude used to help you plan the campaign. Now it can help make the image, the cartoon, and the video, so let me show you what this means for a real business.
ideas.why: The evidence is directly from Braydon's own account, and the current winner is the most practical Claude creative workflow in the last 48 hours.

ideas.title: The 10 finance agents your accountant should know about
ideas.about: Use the competitor signal as a plain-English breakdown, not a fear angle. Show 3 of the most useful Claude finance agents for small business owners: statement review, invoice cleanup, and cash-flow questions.
ideas.evidence: nocode.joshua's May 16 post "Claude just killed the Finance Industry" reached 201.3K views. Cover OCR succeeded with the title "Claude just killed the Finance Industry". Subtitle links were missing, so spoken hook extraction was unavailable and the caption/performance layer is the main evidence.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Claude can read your finances now
ideas.spokenHook: This does not mean Claude replaces your accountant. It means small business owners can finally ask better questions before the meeting.
ideas.why: This is the clearest fresh 10K+ competitor wave from the daily scan, but Braydon can make it safer, more practical, and more human than the competitor's replacement framing.

ideas.title: Move from ChatGPT to Claude without starting over
ideas.about: Show the simple memory/context handoff path between tools, then reframe it as a business continuity workflow: your AI should remember your brand, customers, offers, and constraints.
ideas.evidence: Sabrina Ramonov's May 16 ChatGPT to Claude migration post has 28.1K views, and her spoken hook was extracted: "Here's how to move from ChatGPT to Claude in four simple clicks." Taki's Claude handoff/memory post is still performing at 26.3K views with 960 saves and 343 comments.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Move your ChatGPT brain to Claude
ideas.spokenHook: If you want to try Claude but you do not want to start from scratch, this is the cleanest way to bring your context with you.
ideas.why: This combines fresh migration interest with Braydon's proven Claude education cluster and prior context-file content.

ideas.title: The beginner-friendly AI agent that actually does the boring work
ideas.about: Explain the new wave of no-setup agents in plain language: they connect to Gmail, calendar, docs, tasks, and posting tools, but Braydon shows the safe first workflow instead of hyping autonomous everything.
ideas.evidence: Marcinteodoru's May 15/16 AI SuperAgents post hit 31.1K views. Cover OCR extracted "Ai SuperAgents are LIVE! No more 14 hours Openclaw and Hermes Stress." Rachel Woods' recent operator posts show the same theme from the business operations angle, with one May 15 post at 2.1K views.
ideas.spokenHook: If AI agents feel too technical, start here. Give one agent one boring job, then make it prove itself before you trust it with anything bigger.
ideas.why: Agents are moving from coder-only to beginner-friendly. Braydon's mentor voice can make the shift useful without sounding like tool hype.

ideas.title: Stop using AI like a yes-man
ideas.about: Teach a custom instruction that makes ChatGPT or Claude challenge weak ideas, check missing info, and protect the user from lazy answers.
ideas.evidence: Sabrina's May 15 post on making ChatGPT push back reached 23.5K views. Spoken hook extracted: "ChatGPT just agrees with everything you say. But this one prompt fixes that forever.
ideas.onScreenTitle: Make AI push back on you
ideas.spokenHook: If your AI agrees with every idea you have, it is not helping enough. Add this one instruction and make it think with you.
ideas.why: It is simple, useful, beginner-friendly, and fits Braydon's mentor positioning: AI should extend your expertise, not flatter you.

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "JOB" and I'll send you all the prompts 👇 I'm seeing thousands of AI resumes. Most still fail. Not because they're bad. Because they don't speak the company's language back to the recruiter. Here's the 4-prompt chain 1000+ people used to fix it: Prompt 1: Extract the company's language for skills, outcomes, and traits. Rank by importance. Map each to your closest bullet. Flag gaps. Prompt 2: Ask up to 3 clarifying questions, then rewrite your bullets using the company's exact language without lying about anything. Each bullet under 20 words, metric-driven. Prompt 3: Score your resume vs. the job description. Keyword match, skills, outcomes, role fit. Give a percentage. List missing high-priority terms. Tell me exactly how to hit 80%. Bonus prompt: Act as a hiring manager reviewing my resume for 10 seconds. Would you interview me? What's your biggest doubt and how do I fix it? Never lie on your resume. But this process shows you exactly how recruiters read yours. #resumetips #jobsearch #chatgptprompts #aitips #careertips
competitors.topic: How to beat 1000+ other job applicants ethically w/o lying 🤔
competitors.onScreenTitle: How to beat 1000+ other job applicants ethically w/o lying 🤔
competitors.spokenHook: I'm seeing thousands of AI resumes. Here is the only prompt chain that makes yours actually pass.

competitors.creator: sabrina_ramonov
competitors.text: Follow and comment "PROMPT" and I'll send you hundreds of ChatGPT prompts 👇 ChatGPT agrees with everything you say by default. This 1 custom instruction changes everything. Go to Settings > Personalization > Custom Instructions and paste this: "Prioritize critical thinking by identifying flaws, bias, or missing data in everything I say. Always offer the unspoken truth to make me think sharper." Now ChatGPT challenges you instead of flattering you. Have you ever noticed ChatGPT telling you what you want to hear? What would happen if your AI pushed back on your ideas? Want ChatGPT to make you smarter instead of agreeing with you? #chatgptprompts #chatgpt #ai #productivity #aitools
competitors.topic: How to force ChatGPT to challenge you 🤫

competitors.spokenHook: ChatGPT just agrees with everything you say. But this one prompt fixes that forever.

competitors.spokenHook: Claude forgets everything after the first 20 messages. Here's the fastest fix so in thropic the creators of Claude advise that after 20 or 30 messages with Claude you start a brand new conversation but the problem is when you transfer to a new conversation sometimes Claude leaves out important details and so I I created a new skill called the handoff skill that makes sure that Claude captures all the important information and seamlessly transfers it to any new conversation and this handoff skill generates the summary using the exact format below and it's able to capture the core contexts key decisions what's pending and the tone and style of that conversation and then what it does is that it creates this beautiful hand off summary that it can feed directly inside of the next Claude conversation so now whenever you have a really long conversation inside of Claude all you need to do is type in this skill forward slash hand off and it will generate this perfect hand off summary that you can then copy and put into a brand new conversation with Claude to ensure that you start exactly where you left off without losing any context at all so if you want this exact handoff skill and a full guide on the best Claude skills and how to optimize your Claude setup just comment the word Claude and I'll go ahead and send it over to you and as always if you like this sort Of content and you want to stay ahead in the world of AI, make sure that you give me a follow.

briefMarkdown:
# TikTok Ideas for Braydon Carter - 2026-05-17

Generated: 2026-05-17 6:37 AM MDT

Source of truth: Hermes-native workspace `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/`

## Best ideas for today

**Claude is becoming the creative department** `🔁 V2/reframe` `🌊 wave-ride`
- **What it's about:** Turn yesterday's Claude plus Higgsfield/image momentum into a simple business demo: Claude creates the campaign brief, image asset, and cartoon/video variation for a real small business.
- **Evidence:** Braydon's May 15 Claude plus Higgsfield post is now at 95.8K views, up from 26.5K in the prior brief, with the newer Claude cartoon video already at 27.9K views, 1,098 likes, and 309 shares from May 16 night MT. Justyn's Higgsfield MCP plus Claude post is still a strong wave signal at 57.3K views.
- **On-screen title:** Claude is your creative department now
- **Spoken hook:** Claude used to help you plan the campaign. Now it can help make the image, the cartoon, and the video, so let me show you what this means for a real business.
- **Content type:** tool breakdown
- **Why this should work:** The evidence is directly from Braydon's own account, and the current winner is the most practical Claude creative workflow in the last 48 hours.

**The 10 finance agents your accountant should know about** `🌊 wave-ride`
- **What it's about:** Use the competitor signal as a plain-English breakdown, not a fear angle. Show 3 of the most useful Claude finance agents for small business owners: statement review, invoice cleanup, and cash-flow questions.
- **Evidence:** nocode.joshua's May 16 post "Claude just killed the Finance Industry" reached 201.3K views. Cover OCR succeeded with the title "Claude just killed the Finance Industry". Subtitle links were missing, so spoken hook extraction was unavailable and the caption/performance layer is the main evidence.
- **On-screen title:** Claude can read your finances now
- **Spoken hook:** This does not mean Claude replaces your accountant. It means small business owners can finally ask better questions before the meeting.
- **Content type:** tutorial
- **Why this should work:** This is the clearest fresh 10K+ competitor wave from the daily scan, but Braydon can make it safer, more practical, and more human than the competitor's replacement framing.

**Move from ChatGPT to Claude without starting over** `🌊 wave-ride` `🔁 V2/reframe`
- **What it's about:** Show the simple memory/context handoff path between tools, then reframe it as a business continuity workflow: your AI should remember your brand, customers, offers, and constraints.
- **Evidence:** Sabrina Ramonov's May 16 ChatGPT to Claude migration post has 28.1K views, and her spoken hook was extracted: "Here's how to move from ChatGPT to Claude in four simple clicks." Taki's Claude handoff/memory post is still performing at 26.3K views with 960 saves and 343 comments.
- **On-screen title:** Move your ChatGPT brain to Claude
- **Spoken hook:** If you want to try Claude but you do not want to start from scratch, this is the cleanest way to bring your context with you.
- **Content type:** quick tip
- **Why this should work:** This combines fresh migration interest with Braydon's proven Claude education cluster and prior context-file content.

**The beginner-friendly AI agent that actually does the boring work** `🌊 wave-ride` `📰 news`
- **What it's about:** Explain the new wave of no-setup agents in plain language: they connect to Gmail, calendar, docs, tasks, and posting tools, but Braydon shows the safe first workflow instead of hyping autonomous everything.
- **Evidence:** Marcinteodoru's May 15/16 AI SuperAgents post hit 31.1K views. Cover OCR extracted "Ai SuperAgents are LIVE! No more 14 hours Openclaw and Hermes Stress." Rachel Woods' recent operator posts show the same theme from the business operations angle, with one May 15 post at 2.1K views.
- **On-screen title:** Your first useful AI agent
- **Spoken hook:** If AI agents feel too technical, start here. Give one agent one boring job, then make it prove itself before you trust it with anything bigger.
- **Content type:** myth-bust
- **Why this should work:** Agents are moving from coder-only to beginner-friendly. Braydon's mentor voice can make the shift useful without sounding like tool hype.

**Stop using AI like a yes-man** `🌊 wave-ride`
- **What it's about:** Teach a custom instruction that makes ChatGPT or Claude challenge weak ideas, check missing info, and protect the user from lazy answers.
- **Evidence:** Sabrina's May 15 post on making ChatGPT push back reached 23.5K views. Spoken hook extracted: "ChatGPT just agrees with everything you say. But this one prompt fixes that forever."
- **On-screen title:** Make AI push back on you
- **Spoken hook:** If your AI agrees with every idea you have, it is not helping enough. Add this one instruction and make it think with you.
- **Content type:** quick tip
- **Why this should work:** It is simple, useful, beginner-friendly, and fits Braydon's mentor positioning: AI should extend your expertise, not flatter you.

## Research notes

### Braydon performance
- Braydon scrape succeeded with 17 non-pinned recent videos returned in the compact artifact.
- Top current performer: Claude plus Higgsfield at 95.8K views. This appears to be the strongest still-climbing owned signal from the week.
- Newest strong post: Claude cartoon videos, created 2026-05-16 at about 10:00 PM MT, is already at 27.9K views with 309 shares.
- Pattern working on Braydon's account: concrete Claude output demos beat abstract AI commentary. The winners show Claude making images, websites, courses, videos, or coding workflows.

### Competitor scan
- Daily Success Scan scraped 47 items across all 10 configured competitors after retrying missing handles.
- Recent 10K+ source signals: nocode.joshua finance agents at 201.3K views, Sabrina ChatGPT to Claude migration at 28.1K, Sabrina AI resume prompt chain at 25.3K, Sabrina ChatGPT pushback instruction at 23.5K, Marcinteodoru AI SuperAgents at 31.1K, BrandNat one-person company/GStack at 275.2K, Justyn Higgsfield MCP plus Claude at 57.3K.
- Stale or older high performers were treated as context only, not primary evidence, including older Riley OpenClaw and Tony Waymo posts.

### Early-catch news
- X scan pulled 80 recent AI posts. Strong official signals: Anthropic's US/China AI competition paper, the Gates Foundation partnership with $200M in grants/Claude credits/technical support, and continued OpenAI/Codex chatter.
- Best TikTok fit from news is not geopolitical AI competition. The practical angle is Claude credits, AI access, and AI tools becoming infrastructure for education, health, nonprofits, and small operators.

## Extraction coverage

- Braydon scrape/metrics: success, 17 items returned.
- Braydon on-screen title coverage: 15/17 cached or extracted. New cover OCR completed for the Claude cartoon video.
- Braydon spoken hook coverage: 13/17 from WebVTT/subtitles or existing cache. Missing subtitle links blocked spoken-hook extraction on the newest Claude cartoon video and a few others. Paid transcription was not used.
- Competitor scrape/metrics: success, 47 items across all 10 target handles after retry.
- Competitor on-screen title coverage: 23/47 cached or extracted. Cover OCR was completed for evidence posts that had usable downloaded covers. Some covers were missing or not needed because cached titles already existed.
- Competitor spoken hook coverage: 19/47 cached or extracted from WebVTT/subtitle links. Several recent posts had missing subtitleLinks, so those are caption/performance signals rather than verified spoken-hook signals.
- Evidence-layer note: every competitor post used for an idea has metrics and caption evidence, and title/hook extraction was attempted or found in cache. Where subtitle links were missing, that limitation is stated in the idea evidence.

## Saved artifacts

- Idea file: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/ideas/2026-05-17-tiktok-ideas.md`
- Run artifacts: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/tmp/2026-05-17-1235-cron-run/`
- Hook cache updated: `/opt/data/hermes-content/tiktok/data/tiktok-hooks-cache.json`
